message:
fix(providers): emit ToolCallEnd events for Azure and OpenAI streaming

Both the Azure and OpenAI streaming state machines were missing ToolCallEnd
event emission after finalizing tool call arguments on stream completion.
This caused downstream consumers (extension dispatcher, session logger) to
never receive definitive notification that a tool call's arguments were
fully assembled, leading to potential state machine hangs or missed tool
invocations.

Changes:
- Azure: iterate accumulated tool_calls after finalize_tool_call_arguments()
  and emit ToolCallEnd with content_index, cloned ToolCall, and partial
  AssistantMessage for each completed call; suppress clippy::too_many_lines
  on the already-long process_event method
- OpenAI: identical ToolCallEnd emission loop after finalize, matching the
  existing Anthropic provider behavior
- Gemini: add pending_events VecDeque field to GeminiStream for parity with
  Azure/OpenAI streaming infrastructure (event buffering prep)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
